  "text": "The Grafana codebase (sg-evals/grafana--26d36ec) contains the following key files implementing both the legacy and unified alerting systems:\n\n## Legacy Alert Rule Storage\n\n`pkg/services/sqlstore/migrations/alert_mig.go` — Defines and manages the legacy alerting database schema via `addAlertMigrations`. Creates the core legacy tables: `alert` (dashboard-linked alert rules with columns for dashboard_id, panel_id, settings, state, frequency), `alert_rule_tag`, `alert_notification`, `alert_notification_state`, and `alert_notification_journal`. This is the storage layer for the old, pre-unified alerting engine. Note: The legacy alerting engine runtime code (pkg/services/alerting/) was removed in Grafana 11, but the schema migrations remain.\n\n## Unified Alerting (ngalert) Engine\n\n`pkg/services/ngalert/ngalert.go` — The main `AlertNG` struct and service that implements the unified alerting engine. It runs the scheduler, state manager, and alertmanager components.\n\n`pkg/services/ngalert/store/database.go` — Defines the `DBstore` struct (the central unified alerting storage implementation) and the `AlertingStore` interface for Alertmanager configuration storage.\n\n`pkg/services/ngalert/store/alert_rule.go` — Implements CRUD operations for unified alert rules in the `DBstore`, including `DeleteAlertRulesByUID`, `GetAlertRuleByUID`, `InsertAlertRules`, `UpdateAlertRules`, etc.\n\n`pkg/services/ngalert/models/alert_rule.go` — The `AlertRule` model for unified alerting, including constants like `MigratedLabelPrefix = \"__legacy_\"` that mark rules migrated from legacy alerting.\n\n## Migration Script (Legacy → Unified)\n\n`pkg/services/sqlstore/migrations/ualert/ualert.go` — The primary migration script. Contains `var migTitle = \"move dashboard alerts to unified alerting\"` and the `upgradeNgAlerting` struct which implements the `FixEarlyMigration` (for pre-8.2 unified alerting configs). Also contains `MigrationError` used during migration failures and `updateDashboardUIDPanelIDMigration` to backfill dashboard_uid/panel_id from annotations on unified alert rules.\n\n`pkg/services/sqlstore/migrations/ualert/tables.go` — Defines all unified alerting database tables via `AddTablesMigrations` and `historicalTableMigrations`. Creates tables: `alert_definition`, `alert_rule`, `alert_rule_version`, `alert_instance`, `alert_configuration`, `alert_configuration_history`, and provisioning tables.\n\n`pkg/services/sqlstore/migrations/ualert/migration_service_migration.go` — `MigrationServiceMigration` moves the legacy alert migration status from `migration_log` to the `kv_store` table. Uses `KVNamespace = \"ngalert.migration\"` as the namespace.\n\n`pkg/services/sqlstore/migrations/ualert/org_upgrade_state_mig.go` — `CreateOrgMigratedKVStoreEntries` tracks per-org migration state in kvstore, distinguishing between `Legacy` and `UnifiedAlerting` alerting types. Used to detect transitions and prevent double-migration.\n\n`pkg/services/sqlstore/migrations/ualert/created_folders_mig.go` — `CreatedFoldersMigration` moves the record of folders created during the legacy→unified migration from Dashboard.created_by=-8 sentinel values into the kv_store.\n\n`pkg/services/sqlstore/migrations/migrations.go` — The top-level `OSSMigrations.AddMigration` function that orchestrates all migrations, calling both `addAlertMigrations` (legacy) and all `ualert.*` migrations (unified) in the correct order."
}

# Grafana Legacy Alerting to Unified Alerting Migration

## Your Task

Find all Go source files in grafana/grafana that implement both the legacy alerting engine and the unified alerting (ngalert) engine: the legacy alert rule storage, the unified alert rule storage, and the migration script that converts legacy rules to unified format.

## Context

You are working on a codebase task involving repos from the migration domain.

## Key Files Found

### Legacy Alert Rule Storage
- **`pkg/services/sqlstore/migrations/alert_mig.go`** — Creates the legacy `alert` table (and related tables: `alert_rule_tag`, `alert_notification`, `alert_notification_state`) that backed the old dashboard-linked alerting engine. The legacy runtime engine itself was removed in Grafana 11.

### Unified Alerting (ngalert) Engine
- **`pkg/services/ngalert/ngalert.go`** — The `AlertNG` service (unified alerting engine with scheduler, state manager, alertmanager)

- **`pkg/services/ngalert/store/alert_rule.go`** — CRUD operations for unified alert rules
- **`pkg/services/ngalert/models/alert_rule.go`** — `AlertRule` model with migration label constants like `MigratedLabelPrefix = "__legacy_"`

### Migration Script (Legacy → Unified)
- **`pkg/services/sqlstore/migrations/ualert/ualert.go`** — Primary migration ("move dashboard alerts to unified alerting"), contains `upgradeNgAlerting` and `MigrationError`

- **`pkg/services/sqlstore/migrations/migrations.go`** — Top-level migration orchestrator

11: The current alerting system doesn't support high availability. Alert notifications are not deduplicated and load balancing is not supported between instances; for example, silences from one instance will not appear in the other.
12: 
13: ## Alert evaluation
14: 
15: Grafana managed alerts are evaluated by the Grafana backend. Rule evaluations are scheduled, according to the alert rule configuration, and queries are evaluated by an engine that is part of core Grafana.
16: 
17: Alerting rules can only query backend data sources with alerting enabled:
18: 
19: ","endLine":19,"startLine":10},{"content":"20:   `Google Cloud Monitoring`, `Cloudwatch`, `Azure Monitor`, `MySQL`, `PostgreSQL`, `MSSQL`, `OpenTSDB`, `Oracle`, and `Azure Data Explorer`
21: - any community backend data sources with alerting enabled (`backend` and `alerting` properties are set in the [plugin.json](https://grafana.com/developers/plugin-tools/reference/plugin-json)
22:

48: func ProvideDBStore(
49: 	cfg *setting.Cfg,
50: 	featureToggles featuremgmt.FeatureToggles,
51: 	sqlstore db.DB,
52: 	folderService folder.Service,
53: 	dashboards dashboards.DashboardService,
54: 	ac accesscontrol.AccessControl,
55: 	bus bus.Bus,
56: ) (*DBstore, error) {
57: 	store := DBstore{
58: 		Cfg:              cfg.UnifiedAlerting,
59: 		FeatureToggles:   featureToggles,
60: 		SQLStore:         sqlstore,
61: 		Logger:           log.New("ngalert.dbstore"),
62: 		FolderService:    folderService,
63: 		DashboardService: dashboards,
64: 		AccessControl:    ac,
65: 		Bus:              bus,
66: 	}
67: 	if err := folderService.RegisterService(store); err != nil {
68: 		return nil, err
69: 	}
70: 	return &store, nil
71: }
